highlights.text: I wrote in yesterday’s newsletter: “My lean is bulls can reclaim 6638-43 or 6650 to try and pop to 6664, 6685, then 6701-03.
This squeeze - as impressive as it was - did not last long and this morning ES put in its most vicious elevator down sell since October 10th and the second largest since the April collapse.
These siblings are: 1) Elevator down sell.
Elevator down sells are always followed by the second sibling: The short squeeze.
These are caused by my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
After rallying to 6701 yesterday morning we went elevator down to 6623 by 12:40PM prior to Nvidia earnings.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the two Failed Breakdowns that set the stage for yesterday’s squeeze (this is important to know) and I’ll discuss the actionable trade plan for tomorrow.

post_title: Second Largest Daily Sell For SPX Since April. Will Bulls Be Able To Buy It? November 21 Plan
